sections.action: Validates model. No deployment change needed.

sections.findings: MI+DC+Elo: 1217 bets / +3.7% ROI / +45.5u vs MI-only: 1092 / +2.9% / +32.1u. Elo smooths MI noise, +11% more qualifying bets at same CLV.
Model 6.9% overconfident (MACE=6.9%). Scalar correction hurts: shrink 0.85 -> CLV 10.4%, 0.95 -> CLV 10.8% vs baseline 11.0%. Edge comes from extreme estimates.
sections.action: Do NOT apply uniform shrinkage. Explore isotonic regression later.
Led directly to late-season filter test.

sections.findings: +13.9% ROI vs +2.4% baseline (+11.5pp). Bootstrap p=0.005, 95% CI [+3.3%, +24.8%] excludes zero. Hit rate 58.7% vs 52.6%.
sections.action: Deploy as soft filter (reduced stake GW 30+).

sections.findings: -85 bets, same CLV. MI lambdas already a better expected-goals baseline than raw xG.
CLV identical at +11.0% across all 6 combos (a=6/8 x d=10/15/20). No evidence attack regresses faster than defense.
CLV stable at ~11.0% across ALL windows (6/8/10/15/30/expanding). Window size affects selectivity not edge quality.
CLV barely moves. AND logic removes profitable single-variance bets without improving per-bet quality. ROI degrades as gap tightens.
+4.3% ROI (889 bets) but bootstrap p=0.093, CI includes zero. Not statistically significant.
+2.7% ROI (452 bets) vs +3.1% baseline (725 bets). Worse across all thresholds.
Block bootstrap p=0.118, CI [-2.7%, +11.4%]. Neither variant distinguishable from noise.
All leagues within 1pp CLV (10.4%-11.1%). Serie B ROI strong but CLV comparable. Soft market effect is in ROI not CLV.
Every Benter weight hurts. No-blend baseline is best: CLV +11.0%, ROI +2.6%, 1401 bets.
Only 7% of bets have opening odds. 3 bets with line move >= 3pp. Cannot test until opening odds coverage > 80%.
